117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}

134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({

45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47

49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52

54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined

56	        : inferImportFileExtension({

59	            target,

64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })

94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'

## Monorepo Package Structure Patterns - Complete Report

I've explored the Prisma monorepo structure and gathered all the information you need to create a new package. Here's the complete breakdown:

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

# Plan: New Package `packages/generator-rest-client`

## Context

The task is to add a new Prisma generator package (`@prisma/generator-rest-client`) to the monorepo that reads the DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) from a Prisma schema and emits a typed REST API client as a single `rest-client.ts` file. This follows the same generator protocol used by `@prisma/generator-helper` — the generator runs as a standalone Node.js binary that communicates with the Prisma CLI over stdin/stderr JSON-RPC 2.0.

Users configure it in their schema:
```prisma
generator restClient {
  provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
  output   = "./generated/rest-client"
}
```

---

## Critical Files to Create

```
packages/generator-rest-client/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts        ← entry point + generatorHandler registration
│   ├── generator.ts    ← main generation logic (DMMF → file content)
│   └── template.ts     ← string template helpers
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts        ← build config (custom, adds #!/usr/bin/env node shebang)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── tsconfig.build.json
```

One existing file to modify:
- `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` — add `"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]` to `compilerOptions.paths`

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — `package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",  "default": "./dist/index.js"  },
      "import":  { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" }
    }
  },
  "bin": {
    "prisma-generator-rest-client": "dist/index.js"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/generator-rest-client"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev":            "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build":          "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

Key points:
- `bin` field maps `prisma-generator-rest-client` → `dist/index.js` so Prisma CLI can spawn it
- Only three workspace deps needed (dmmf for types, generator for protocol types, generator-helper for `generatorHandler`)

### Step 2 — `tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

### Step 3 — `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### Step 4 — `helpers/build.ts`

Use `createAdapterConfig` (not the pre-built `adapterConfig`) so we can add a `banner` with the shebang to the CJS bundle — required for the `bin` entry point to be directly executable on Unix:

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { createAdapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

const [cjsBuild, esmBuild] = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])

void build([
  { ...cjsBuild, banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' } },
  esmBuild,
])
```

### Step 5 — `src/template.ts`

Pure helper functions; no imports needed from Prisma packages:

```typescript
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'

/** Maps Prisma scalar field types to TypeScript types. */
export function scalarToTs(prismaType: string): string {
  switch (prismaType) {
    case 'String':   return 'string'
    case 'Boolean':  return 'boolean'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
    case 'Decimal':  return 'number'
    case 'BigInt':   return 'bigint'
    case 'DateTime': return 'Date'
    case 'Json':     return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':    return 'Uint8Array'
    default:         return prismaType  // enum or other model name
  }
}

/** Returns the TypeScript type string for a DMMF field (scalar + list + optional). */
export function fieldToTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string {
  if (field.kind === 'object') return null  // skip relation fields (handled separately)
  const base = scalarToTs(field.type) + (field.isList ? '[]' : '')
  return field.isRequired ? base : `${base} | null`
}

/** Converts a PascalCase model name to a camelCase resource key. */
export function toCamelCase(name: string): string {
  return name.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)
}

/** Emits a TypeScript interface for one model (scalar fields only). */
export function renderModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }

/** Emits the Args interfaces for a model (FindManyArgs, FindUniqueArgs, CreateArgs, UpdateArgs, DeleteArgs). */
export function renderArgsInterfaces(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }

/** Emits the resource object literal type and implementation for one model. */
export function renderModelResource(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }
```

`fieldToTsType` returns `null` for `kind === 'object'` (relation fields) — the generator skips those when building interfaces so the output stays portable without knowledge of the server-side join logic.

### Step 6 — `src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import fs from 'node:fs'
import path from 'node:path'
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { renderModelInterface, renderArgsInterfaces, renderModelResource, toCamelCase } from './template'

export async function generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
  const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
  if (!outputDir) throw new Error('output directory is required')

  fs.mkdirSync(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const { models } = options.dmmf.datamodel
  const lines: string[] = ['// Generated by @prisma/generator-rest-client — do not edit', '']

  for (const model of models) {
    lines.push(renderModelInterface(model))
    lines.push(renderArgsInterfaces(model))
  }

  // createRestClient factory function
  lines.push('export function createRestClient(baseUrl: string) {')
  lines.push('  return {')
  for (const model of models) {
    lines.push(renderModelResource(model))
  }
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('}')

  const outFile = path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts')
  fs.writeFileSync(outFile, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
}
```

**DMMF traversal details:**
- `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` — array of `DMMF.Model`
- `model.fields` filtered by `field.kind !== 'object'` for scalar/enum fields
- `field.isId` to find the identifier field (for `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` where clause)
- `field.hasDefaultValue` or `field.isId` to mark fields optional in `CreateArgs`

**Generated output shape per model (example for `User`):**
```typescript
export interface User {
  id: number
  email: string
  name: string | null
}

export interface UserFindManyArgs {
  where?: Partial<User>
  take?: number
  skip?: number
  orderBy?: Partial<Record<keyof User, 'asc' | 'desc'>>
}

export interface UserFindUniqueArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
}

export interface UserCreateArgs {
  data: Omit<User, 'id'>
}

export interface UserUpdateArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
  data: Partial<Omit<User, 'id'>>
}

export interface UserDeleteArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
}
```

The `createRestClient` factory returns an object with one key per model (camelCase) whose methods (`findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) call `fetch` with standard REST conventions:
- `GET  /users`            — findMany
- `GET  /users/:id`        — findUnique
- `POST /users`            — create
- `PUT  /users/:id`        — update
- `DELETE /users/:id`      — delete

Each method returns the appropriately typed `Promise<T>` or `Promise<T[]>`.

### Step 7 — `src/index.ts`

```typescript
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generator'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client Generator',
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
    }
  },
  async onGenerate(options) {
    await generate(options)
  },
})
```

`generatorHandler` wires up stdin/stderr JSON-RPC automatically (from `@prisma/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`). No additional protocol handling is needed.

### Step 8 — Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add one line to `compilerOptions.paths` (after the existing `@prisma/generator-helper` entry, line ~21):

```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"],
```

---

## Key Reused Utilities

| Symbol | Source file |
|--------|-------------|
| `generatorHandler` | `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` |
| `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorManifest` | `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` (re-exported by `@prisma/generator-helper`) |
| `DMMF.Model`, `DMMF.Field` | `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` |
| `createAdapterConfig` | `helpers/compile/configs.ts` |
| `build` | `helpers/compile/build.ts` |

---

## Verification

1. **Build the package:**
   ```sh
   pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build
   ```
   Expected: `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.d.ts` created; `dist/index.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`.

2. **Confirm binary is wired up:**
   ```sh
   node packages/generator-rest-client/dist/index.js
   ```
   Process should hang waiting for stdin (generator protocol). `Ctrl+C` to exit — no crash means the handler started correctly.

3. **Integration test with a sample schema** (requires `prisma` CLI):
   Create a temporary `schema.prisma` + `prisma.config.ts` with:
   ```prisma
   generator restClient {
     provider = "node packages/generator-rest-client/dist/index.js"
     output   = "./generated/rest-client"
   }
   model User {
     id    Int    @id @default(autoincrement())
     email String @unique
     name  String?
   }
   ```
   Run `pnpm prisma generate --schema=<path>`. Inspect `./generated/rest-client/rest-client.ts` for correct interfaces and `createRestClient` factory.

4. **TypeScript check on generated output:**
   ```sh
   npx tsc --noEmit generated/rest-client/rest-client.ts
   ```
   Should pass with no errors.

### Step 2 — `tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

### Step 3 — `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### Step 4 — `helpers/build.ts`

Use `createAdapterConfig` (not the pre-built `adapterConfig`) so we can add a `banner` with the shebang to the CJS bundle — required for the `bin` entry point to be directly executable on Unix:

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { createAdapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

const [cjsBuild, esmBuild] = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])

void build([
  { ...cjsBuild, banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' } },
  esmBuild,
])
```

### Step 5 — `src/template.ts`

Pure helper functions; no imports needed from Prisma packages:

```typescript
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'

/** Maps Prisma scalar field types to TypeScript types. */
export function scalarToTs(prismaType: string): string {
  switch (prismaType) {
    case 'String':   return 'string'
    case 'Boolean':  return 'boolean'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
    case 'Decimal':  return 'number'
    case 'BigInt':   return 'bigint'
    case 'DateTime': return 'Date'
    case 'Json':     return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':    return 'Uint8Array'
    default:         return prismaType  // enum or other model name
  }
}

/** Returns the TypeScript type string for a DMMF field (scalar + list + optional). */
export function fieldToTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string {
  if (field.kind === 'object') return null  // skip relation fields (handled separately)
  const base = scalarToTs(field.type) + (field.isList ? '[]' : '')
  return field.isRequired ? base : `${base} | null`
}

/** Converts a PascalCase model name to a camelCase resource key. */
export function toCamelCase(name: string): string {
  return name.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)
}

/** Emits a TypeScript interface for one model (scalar fields only). */
export function renderModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }

/** Emits the Args interfaces for a model (FindManyArgs, FindUniqueArgs, CreateArgs, UpdateArgs, DeleteArgs). */
export function renderArgsInterfaces(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }

/** Emits the resource object literal type and implementation for one model. */
export function renderModelResource(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }
```

`fieldToTsType` returns `null` for `kind === 'object'` (relation fields) — the generator skips those when building interfaces so the output stays portable without knowledge of the server-side join logic.

### Step 6 — `src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import fs from 'node:fs'
import path from 'node:path'
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { renderModelInterface, renderArgsInterfaces, renderModelResource, toCamelCase } from './template'

export async function generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
  const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
  if (!outputDir) throw new Error('output directory is required')

  fs.mkdirSync(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const { models } = options.dmmf.datamodel
  const lines: string[] = ['// Generated by @prisma/generator-rest-client — do not edit', '']

  for (const model of models) {
    lines.push(renderModelInterface(model))
    lines.push(renderArgsInterfaces(model))
  }

  // createRestClient factory function
  lines.push('export function createRestClient(baseUrl: string) {')
  lines.push('  return {')
  for (const model of models) {
    lines.push(renderModelResource(model))
  }
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('}')

  const outFile = path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts')
  fs.writeFileSync(outFile, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
}
```

**DMMF traversal details:**
- `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` — array of `DMMF.Model`
- `model.fields` filtered by `field.kind !== 'object'` for scalar/enum fields
- `field.isId` to find the identifier field (for `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` where clause)

```typescript
export interface User {
  id: number
  email: string
  name: string | null
}

export interface UserFindManyArgs {
  where?: Partial<User>
  take?: number
  skip?: number
  orderBy?: Partial<Record<keyof User, 'asc' | 'desc'>>
}

export interface UserFindUniqueArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
}

export interface UserCreateArgs {
  data: Omit<User, 'id'>
}

export interface UserUpdateArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
  data: Partial<Omit<User, 'id'>>
}

export interface UserDeleteArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
}
```

The `createRestClient` factory returns an object with one key per model (camelCase) whose methods (`findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) call `fetch` with standard REST conventions:
- `GET  /users`            — findMany
- `GET  /users/:id`        — findUnique
- `POST /users`            — create
- `PUT  /users/:id`        — update
- `DELETE /users/:id`      — delete

Each method returns the appropriately typed `Promise<T>` or `Promise<T[]>`.

### Step 7 — `src/index.ts`

```typescript
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generator'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client Generator',
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
    }
  },
  async onGenerate(options) {
    await generate(options)
  },
})
```

`generatorHandler` wires up stdin/stderr JSON-RPC automatically (from `@prisma/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`). No additional protocol handling is needed.

### Step 8 — Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add one line to `compilerOptions.paths` (after the existing `@prisma/generator-helper` entry, line ~21):

```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"],
```

---

|--------|-------------|
| `generatorHandler` | `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` |
| `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorManifest` | `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` (re-exported by `@prisma/generator-helper`) |
| `DMMF.Model`, `DMMF.Field` | `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` |
| `createAdapterConfig` | `helpers/compile/configs.ts` |
| `build` | `helpers/compile/build.ts` |

---

## Verification

1. **Build the package:**
   ```sh

   ```
   Expected: `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.d.ts` created; `dist/index.js` starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`.

2. **Confirm binary is wired up:**
   ```sh

   ```

3. **Integration test with a sample schema** (requires `prisma` CLI):
   Create a temporary `schema.prisma` + `prisma.config.ts` with:
   ```prisma

     provider = "node packages/generator-rest-client/dist/index.js"
     output   = "./generated/rest-client"
   }
   model User {

   }

4. **TypeScript check on generated output:**

   ```

- `bin` field maps `prisma-generator-rest-client` → `dist/index.js` so Prisma CLI can spawn it
- Only three workspace deps needed (dmmf for types, generator for protocol types, generator-helper for `generatorHandler`)

### Step 2 — `tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

### Step 3 — `tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

### Step 4 — `helpers/build.ts`

Use `createAdapterConfig` (not the pre-built `adapterConfig`) so we can add a `banner` with the shebang to the CJS bundle — required for the `bin` entry point to be directly executable on Unix:

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { createAdapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

const [cjsBuild, esmBuild] = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])

void build([
  { ...cjsBuild, banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' } },
  esmBuild,
])
```

### Step 5 — `src/template.ts`

Pure helper functions; no imports needed from Prisma packages:

```typescript
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'

/** Maps Prisma scalar field types to TypeScript types. */
export function scalarToTs(prismaType: string): string {
  switch (prismaType) {
    case 'String':   return 'string'
    case 'Boolean':  return 'boolean'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
    case 'Decimal':  return 'number'
    case 'BigInt':   return 'bigint'
    case 'DateTime': return 'Date'
    case 'Json':     return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':    return 'Uint8Array'
    default:         return prismaType  // enum or other model name
  }
}

/** Returns the TypeScript type string for a DMMF field (scalar + list + optional). */
export function fieldToTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string {
  if (field.kind === 'object') return null  // skip relation fields (handled separately)
  const base = scalarToTs(field.type) + (field.isList ? '[]' : '')
  return field.isRequired ? base : `${base} | null`
}

/** Converts a PascalCase model name to a camelCase resource key. */
export function toCamelCase(name: string): string {
  return name.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)
}

/** Emits a TypeScript interface for one model (scalar fields only). */
export function renderModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }

/** Emits the Args interfaces for a model (FindManyArgs, FindUniqueArgs, CreateArgs, UpdateArgs, DeleteArgs). */
export function renderArgsInterfaces(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }

/** Emits the resource object literal type and implementation for one model. */
export function renderModelResource(model: DMMF.Model): string { ... }
```

`fieldToTsType` returns `null` for `kind === 'object'` (relation fields) — the generator skips those when building interfaces so the output stays portable without knowledge of the server-side join logic.

### Step 6 — `src/generator.ts`

```typescript
import fs from 'node:fs'
import path from 'node:path'
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
import { renderModelInterface, renderArgsInterfaces, renderModelResource, toCamelCase } from './template'

export async function generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
  const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
  if (!outputDir) throw new Error('output directory is required')

  fs.mkdirSync(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const { models } = options.dmmf.datamodel
  const lines: string[] = ['// Generated by @prisma/generator-rest-client — do not edit', '']

  for (const model of models) {
    lines.push(renderModelInterface(model))
    lines.push(renderArgsInterfaces(model))
  }

  // createRestClient factory function
  lines.push('export function createRestClient(baseUrl: string) {')
  lines.push('  return {')
  for (const model of models) {
    lines.push(renderModelResource(model))
  }
  lines.push('  }')
  lines.push('}')

  const outFile = path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts')
  fs.writeFileSync(outFile, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
}
```

**DMMF traversal details:**
- `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` — array of `DMMF.Model`
- `model.fields` filtered by `field.kind !== 'object'` for scalar/enum fields
- `field.isId` to find the identifier field (for `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` where clause)
- `field.hasDefaultValue` or `field.isId` to mark fields optional in `CreateArgs`

**Generated output shape per model (example for `User`):**
```typescript
export interface User {
  id: number
  email: string
  name: string | null
}

export interface UserFindManyArgs {
  where?: Partial<User>
  take?: number
  skip?: number
  orderBy?: Partial<Record<keyof User, 'asc' | 'desc'>>
}

export interface UserFindUniqueArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
}

export interface UserCreateArgs {
  data: Omit<User, 'id'>
}

export interface UserUpdateArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
  data: Partial<Omit<User, 'id'>>
}

export interface UserDeleteArgs {
  where: Pick<User, 'id'>
}
```

The `createRestClient` factory returns an object with one key per model (camelCase) whose methods (`findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) call `fetch` with standard REST conventions:
- `GET  /users`            — findMany
- `GET  /users/:id`        — findUnique
- `POST /users`            — create
- `PUT  /users/:id`        — update
- `DELETE /users/:id`      — delete

Each method returns the appropriately typed `Promise<T>` or `Promise<T[]>`.

### Step 7 — `src/index.ts`

```typescript
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generator'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest() {
    return {
      prettyName: 'Prisma REST Client Generator',
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
    }
  },
  async onGenerate(options) {
    await generate(options)
  },
})
```

`generatorHandler` wires up stdin/stderr JSON-RPC automatically (from `@prisma/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`). No additional protocol handling is needed.

### Step 8 — Modify `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add one line to `compilerOptions.paths` (after the existing `@prisma/generator-helper` entry, line ~21):

import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/dmmf'

/** Maps a Prisma scalar type name to its TypeScript equivalent. */
export function scalarToTs(prismaType: string): string {
  switch (prismaType) {
    case 'String':
      return 'string'
    case 'Boolean':
      return 'boolean'
    case 'Int':
    case 'Float':
    case 'Decimal':
      return 'number'
    case 'BigInt':
      return 'bigint'
    case 'DateTime':
      return 'Date'
    case 'Json':
      return 'unknown'
    case 'Bytes':
      return 'Uint8Array'
    default:
      return prismaType
  }
}

/**
 * Returns the TypeScript type annotation for a scalar or enum field.
 * Returns null for relation fields (`kind === 'object'`), which the generator skips
 * so the output stays portable without knowledge of server-side join logic.
 */
export function fieldToTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string | null {
  if (field.kind === 'object') return null
  const base = scalarToTs(field.type) + (field.isList ? '[]' : '')
  return field.isRequired ? base : `${base} | null`
}

/** Converts a PascalCase model name to a camelCase property key. */
export function toCamelCase(name: string): string {
  return name.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)
}

/** Returns only the non-relation fields of a model. */
function scalarFields(model: DMMF.Model): readonly DMMF.Field[] {
  return model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind !== 'object')
}

/**
 * Returns the `@id` field of a model, falling back to the first scalar field
 * if no `@id` is present (e.g. composite-key models).
 */
function idField(model: DMMF.Model): DMMF.Field {
  const fields = scalarFields(model)
  return fields.find((f) => f.isId) ?? fields[0]
}

/** Emits a TypeScript interface for one model (scalar and enum fields only). */
export function renderModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const fields = scalarFields(model)
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}: ${fieldToTsType(f)!}`)
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name} {\n${fields}\n}\n`
}

/** Emits the five CRUD Args interfaces for a model. */
export function renderArgsInterfaces(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const { name } = model
  const idName = idField(model).name

  return [
    `export interface ${name}FindManyArgs {`,
    `  where?: Partial<${name}>`,
    `  take?: number`,
    `  skip?: number`,
    `  orderBy?: Partial<Record<keyof ${name}, 'asc' | 'desc'>>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}FindUniqueArgs {`,
    `  where: Pick<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}CreateArgs {`,
    `  data: Omit<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}UpdateArgs {`,
    `  where: Pick<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `  data: Partial<Omit<${name}, '${idName}'>>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}DeleteArgs {`,
    `  where: Pick<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
  ].join('\n')
}

/**
 * Emits the resource object entry for one model inside the `createRestClient`
 * return object. Generates typed `fetch`-based implementations for the five
 * standard CRUD methods.
 */
export function renderModelResource(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const { name } = model
  const key = toCamelCase(name)
  const urlPath = `${key}s`
  const idName = idField(model).name
  const i = '    ' // 4-space indent (inside `return { ... }`)

  return [
    `${i}${key}: {`,
    `${i}  findMany: async (args?: ${name}FindManyArgs): Promise<${name}[]> => {`,
    `${i}    const url = new URL(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}\`)`,
    `${i}    if (args?.take !== undefined) url.searchParams.set('take', String(args.take))`,
    `${i}    if (args?.skip !== undefined) url.searchParams.set('skip', String(args.skip))`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(url.toString())`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}[]>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  findUnique: async (args: ${name}FindUniqueArgs): Promise<${name} | null> => {`,
    `${i}    const id = args.where.${idName}`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}/\${id}\`)`,
    `${i}    if (response.status === 404) return null`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  create: async (args: ${name}CreateArgs): Promise<${name}> => {`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}\`, {`,
    `${i}      method: 'POST',`,
    `${i}      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },`,
    `${i}      body: JSON.stringify(args.data),`,
    `${i}    })`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  update: async (args: ${name}UpdateArgs): Promise<${name}> => {`,
    `${i}    const id = args.where.${idName}`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}/\${id}\`, {`,
    `${i}      method: 'PUT',`,
    `${i}      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },`,
    `${i}      body: JSON.stringify(args.data),`,
    `${i}    })`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  delete: async (args: ${name}DeleteArgs): Promise<${name}> => {`,
    `${i}    const id = args.where.${idName}`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}/\${id}\`, {`,
    `${i}      method: 'DELETE',`,
    `${i}    })`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}},`,
  ].join('\n')
}

import path from 'node:path'

import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'

import { renderArgsInterfaces, renderModelInterface, renderModelResource } from './template'

export async function generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
  const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value
  if (!outputDir) throw new Error('@prisma/generator-rest-client: output directory is required')

  fs.mkdirSync(outputDir, { recursive: true })

  const { models } = options.dmmf.datamodel
  const sections: string[] = ['// Generated by @prisma/generator-rest-client — do not edit\n']

  for (const model of models) {

  sections.push('export function createRestClient(baseUrl: string) {')
  sections.push('  return {')
  for (const model of models) {
    sections.push(renderModelResource(model))
  }
  sections.push('  }')
  sections.push('}')

  const content = sections.join('\n') + '\n'
  fs.writeFileSync(path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts'), content, 'utf-8')
}

export function fieldToTsType(field: DMMF.Field): string | null {
  if (field.kind === 'object') return null
  const base = scalarToTs(field.type) + (field.isList ? '[]' : '')
  return field.isRequired ? base : `${base} | null`
}

/** Converts a PascalCase model name to a camelCase property key. */
export function toCamelCase(name: string): string {
  return name.charAt(0).toLowerCase() + name.slice(1)
}

/** Returns only the non-relation fields of a model. */
function scalarFields(model: DMMF.Model): readonly DMMF.Field[] {
  return model.fields.filter((f) => f.kind !== 'object')
}

/**
 * Returns the `@id` field of a model, falling back to the first scalar field
 * if no `@id` is present (e.g. composite-key models).
 */
function idField(model: DMMF.Model): DMMF.Field {
  const fields = scalarFields(model)
  return fields.find((f) => f.isId) ?? fields[0]
}

/** Emits a TypeScript interface for one model (scalar and enum fields only). */
export function renderModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const fields = scalarFields(model)
    .map((f) => `  ${f.name}: ${fieldToTsType(f)!}`)
    .join('\n')
  return `export interface ${model.name} {\n${fields}\n}\n`
}

/** Emits the five CRUD Args interfaces for a model. */
export function renderArgsInterfaces(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const { name } = model
  const idName = idField(model).name

  return [
    `export interface ${name}FindManyArgs {`,
    `  where?: Partial<${name}>`,
    `  take?: number`,
    `  skip?: number`,
    `  orderBy?: Partial<Record<keyof ${name}, 'asc' | 'desc'>>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}FindUniqueArgs {`,
    `  where: Pick<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}CreateArgs {`,
    `  data: Omit<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}UpdateArgs {`,
    `  where: Pick<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `  data: Partial<Omit<${name}, '${idName}'>>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
    `export interface ${name}DeleteArgs {`,
    `  where: Pick<${name}, '${idName}'>`,
    `}`,
    ``,
  ].join('\n')
}

/**
 * Emits the resource object entry for one model inside the `createRestClient`
 * return object. Generates typed `fetch`-based implementations for the five
 * standard CRUD methods.
 */
export function renderModelResource(model: DMMF.Model): string {
  const { name } = model
  const key = toCamelCase(name)
  const urlPath = `${key}s`
  const idName = idField(model).name
  const i = '    ' // 4-space indent (inside `return { ... }`)

  return [
    `${i}${key}: {`,
    `${i}  findMany: async (args?: ${name}FindManyArgs): Promise<${name}[]> => {`,
    `${i}    const url = new URL(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}\`)`,
    `${i}    if (args?.take !== undefined) url.searchParams.set('take', String(args.take))`,
    `${i}    if (args?.skip !== undefined) url.searchParams.set('skip', String(args.skip))`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(url.toString())`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}[]>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  findUnique: async (args: ${name}FindUniqueArgs): Promise<${name} | null> => {`,
    `${i}    const id = args.where.${idName}`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}/\${id}\`)`,
    `${i}    if (response.status === 404) return null`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  create: async (args: ${name}CreateArgs): Promise<${name}> => {`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}\`, {`,
    `${i}      method: 'POST',`,
    `${i}      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },`,
    `${i}      body: JSON.stringify(args.data),`,
    `${i}    })`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  update: async (args: ${name}UpdateArgs): Promise<${name}> => {`,
    `${i}    const id = args.where.${idName}`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}/\${id}\`, {`,
    `${i}      method: 'PUT',`,
    `${i}      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },`,
    `${i}      body: JSON.stringify(args.data),`,
    `${i}    })`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}  delete: async (args: ${name}DeleteArgs): Promise<${name}> => {`,
    `${i}    const id = args.where.${idName}`,
    `${i}    const response = await fetch(\`\${baseUrl}/${urlPath}/\${id}\`, {`,
    `${i}      method: 'DELETE',`,
    `${i}    })`,
    `${i}    return response.json() as Promise<${name}>`,
    `${i}  },`,
    `${i}},`,
  ].join('\n')
}
